success_criteria: normalize_for_speech never sees bracket characters from vibe tags — strip_vibe_tags runs BEFORE normalization on the non-expressive path
Trailing tags like '[alert] [serious]' are fully removed (brackets AND words) for non-expressive models
Inline tags like 'hello [serious] world' are fully removed for non-expressive models
Leading tags continue to work as before (no regression)
Expressive path preserves tags at any position through normalization (for future re-enablement)
make check passes
Design covers client-side env vars (VOXD_HOST, VOXD_PORT, VOX_AUTH_TOKEN) with exact resolution order and defaults
Design covers server-side VOXD_BIND with default 127.0.0.1 and opt-in to 0.0.0.0
Design is aligned with quarry's remote daemon pattern (read ../quarry for reference)
Design is aligned with lux mcp-proxy-proposal direction (read ../lux/docs/mcp-proxy-proposal.md)
Every changed file is listed with the specific change needed
Security implications of network-exposed voxd are addressed
Design is minimal — no unnecessary abstractions or future-proofing
client.py has _env_host(), _env_port(), _env_token() helpers reading VOXD_HOST, VOXD_PORT, VOXD_TOKEN
VoxClient and VoxClientSync default host uses _env_host()
_resolve_port() checks _env_port() before file fallback
_resolve_token() checks _env_token() before file fallback
voxd.py --host option has envvar="VOXD_BIND"
voxd.py logs WARNING when binding to non-localhost
voxd.py has _TokenRedactFilter that strips ?token= from access log messages
service.py bakes VOXD_BIND into systemd and launchd units when set
__main__.py doctor reports active VOXD_* env var overrides
Tests cover env var resolution, invalid VOXD_PORT, token redaction, bind warning, doctor reporting
make check passes
